$comment: Registry of deliberately forked gate scripts. Each consuming skill carries its own byte-identical copy for substrate independence: Managed-Agent cookbooks bundle skills separately, and ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT} is only guaranteed in hook/MCP contexts — not in skill Bash calls — so share-by-reference is not portable. Duplication is correct; silent DRIFT is the bug. Never hand-edit a copy: edit the canonical, then run `python3 scripts/fork_sync.py --write`. To add a consumer skill, append its copy path here and run --write. check.py (§5) and supply_chain_check.py assert byte-identity from this file in CI.

forks.why: spec hard-gate (H1-H4) runs in interactive /ce-spec and in the auto-build orchestrator; ce-patch imports it by path instead of forking (see check.py §5b)
test gate runs per-task in interactive implement (snapshot mode) and per-feature in the auto-build orchestrator
Scope Lock file-boundary gate: runs in-loop in interactive /ce-implement (Stage 2) and per-run in the auto-build orchestrator (step 5e); also the merge-bar's implement-scope advisory gate
dependency gate: per-task in implement, per-feature in auto-build, cross-feature dependency-manifest backstop in verify
typosquat reference data consumed by dep-guard.py — must ship beside every dep-guard copy

evidence-stamp writer + freshness `check`: `/ce-implement` stamps done-ness, and the three done-ness consumers each run `check` to downgrade a task to `stale` when its commit_sha left HEAD's ancestry — resume (implement), the Stage-0 implemented predicate (verify), and the no-GO-over-unverified rule (ship-release); auto-build carries it as the orchestrator of the implement lane. Each bundles its own copy because CLAUDE_SKILL_DIR is the only path guarantee in skill Bash

evidence compiler: /ce-retro runs it as its aggregation floor, and /ce-ship-release's evidence-pack invokes it as a same-directory sibling subprocess (embedding its JSON), so the pack script needs a byte-identical copy beside it — CLAUDE_SKILL_DIR is the only path guarantee in skill Bash

auditor-consumable evidence pack composer: /ce-retro exports it and /ce-ship-release generates a per-release pack; each bundles its own copy (with its own audit-export.py sibling) because CLAUDE_SKILL_DIR is the only path guarantee in skill Bash

session write lease set/restore for every read-only-on-code skill — the Stage-0/exit halves of the write-scope-guard lease discipline; each skill bundles its own copy because CLAUDE_SKILL_DIR is the only path guarantee in skill Bash

plan structural-integrity lint (H1-H8 + advisories): /ce-plan-audit runs it as its hard-lint floor, /ce-plan runs it as a Stage-9 write-time gate over the just-written plan directory (its ~40-item Validation Checklist is otherwise model-self-attested), and /ce-auto-build plan-lints the resolved plan at Stage-0 kickoff before any spawn so a structurally broken plan parks the run instead of burning spec-agent budget. Each consumer bundles its own copy because CLAUDE_SKILL_DIR is the only path guarantee in skill Bash. Also the merge-bar's plan-lint advisory gate.

the merge bar's integrity runner. The canonical lives at repo-root scripts/ — OUTSIDE the plugin, so an installed plugin never ships it — yet /ce-auto-build's Stage-3 end-review runs it against the isolated checkpoint branch to render the exact CI merge-bar verdict BEFORE the human opens a PR; the plugin therefore forks a byte-identical copy beside the skill (CLAUDE_SKILL_DIR is the only path guarantee in skill Bash). This ONE shared entry carries two copies: the ce-auto-build skill copy AND plugins/core-engineering/mcp/gate_runner.py, forked by WS2-T12's MCP server (mcp/gate_server.py) because the MCP surface ships inside the plugin and CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT never reaches repo-root scripts/. Both copies' DEFAULT_PLUGIN_ROOT mis-resolves from the forked location, so every caller (the skill's Stage-3 step and the MCP server alike) passes explicit --policy/--plugin-root, which keeps the fork byte-identical.

the shared merge-bar finding-disposition ledger reader. Two advisory gates consume it in-place from their own skill dirs — secrets-guard.py (ce-probe-infra, canonical beside it) and sca-guard.py (ce-probe-deps) — and each imports it dir-locally because CLAUDE_SKILL_DIR is the only path guarantee in skill Bash and a merge-bar gate must resolve its imports beside itself. disposition-lint.py (ce-probe-infra) imports the canonical. evidence-pack.py is a third kind of reader — a REPORTER, not a gate: it renders the ledger as the pack's accepted-risk register (active vs expired, never suppressed), so a co-shipped sibling ships beside each of ITS two copies (ce-retro canonical + ce-ship-release fork); portability_check.py's nonstdlib_imports() allows a sibling `<name>.py` import for exactly this. One schema, no share-by-reference; edit the canonical and fork_sync.
